text: Chronological age is one of the strongest risk factors for future calf muscle strain injury.
Previous history of calf strain is one of the strongest risk factors for future calf muscle injury.
Previous lower limb injuries (hamstring, quadriceps, adductor, knee) show limited evidence for an association with future calf strain injury.
Height, weight, gender, and side dominance lack evidence of association with calf strain injury risk.
Older athletes should incorporate targeted calf injury prevention strategies such as progressive calf strengthening and adequate warm-up given their elevated risk.
Athletes with a history of calf strain should undertake specific rehabilitation and prevention programs to reduce recurrence risk.
